channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Dallas Mavericks
channel.description: Dallas Mavericks trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. …
item.description: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić.

item.title: "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr …
item.description: The Warriors remember dealing with it even before the Durant days. Kerr injected an ecosystem of off-ball offensive movement that fully unlocked the shooting greatness of Curry and Klay Thompson. When it vaulted them to the top of the NBA mountain, the outcry was swift and loud: Illegal screens! "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr said. "It did frustrate us. Especially if teams countered that by flopping. You run into a slightly moving screen and act like you've just been hit by

item.title: I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general …
item.description: As far as you personally, what have these last three days been like? You just got hired on Friday. Mike Schmitz: Yeah, it’s been chaotic in a beautiful way. It’s not lost on me what an amazing opportunity this is. I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general manager for such a prestigious organization, working in unison with Masai, obviously Patrick Dumont, and the rest of the ownership group believing in me at a really, really important time — I’m very, very thankful for this chance. I’m su

item.title: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Masai Ujiri: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is Cooper Flagg. Wherever you go around the world, that’s the gem you’re looking for. That’s the type of guy you’re looking for."

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. …
item.description: Those priorities remain, according to league sources. There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić.

item.title: "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr …
item.description: The Warriors remember dealing with it even before the Durant days. Kerr injected an ecosystem of off-ball offensive movement that fully unlocked the shooting greatness of Curry and Klay Thompson. When it vaulted them to the top of the NBA mountain, the outcry was swift and loud: Illegal screens! "That became a constant topic of conversation," Kerr said. "It did frustrate us. Especially if teams countered that by flopping. You run into a slightly moving screen and act like you've just been hit by

item.title: I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general …
item.description: As far as you personally, what have these last three days been like? You just got hired on Friday. Mike Schmitz: Yeah, it’s been chaotic in a beautiful way. It’s not lost on me what an amazing opportunity this is. I’m 35 years old, and to be able to be a general manager for such a prestigious organization, working in unison with Masai, obviously Patrick Dumont, and the rest of the ownership group believing in me at a really, really important time — I’m very, very thankful for this chance. I’m su

item.title: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Masai Ujiri: "We’ve got a guy who makes it easy for us. His name is Cooper Flagg. Wherever you go around the world, that’s the gem you’re looking for. That’s the type of guy you’re looking for."

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the …
item.description: Former Dallas GM Donnie Nelson is leading the consortium behind this move. Luka Doncic is among the investors, while former player Rimantas Kaukenas, who competed in Italy for Siena and Reggio Emilia, is also among the club representatives. What’s interesting in the report is that the club is also presented as interested in a wildcard for the EuroLeague or the EuroCup.

item.title: There is a desire for better center play, true lob …
item.description: There is a desire for better center play, true lob threats who mimic the skills of Daniel Gafford and Dereck Lively II (with cleaner injury histories). There is a desire for true two-way defensive players, wings with athleticism and energy on the defensive end and a sweet shooting stroke on the offensive side of the ball. And there’s a desire to lock in a long-term secondary playmaker next to Dončić. Reaves, who will decline his player option and become an unrestricted free agent after the sea

item.title: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a …
item.description: Jason Kidd: This one hurts. Jason Collins was a pioneer. He had courage like you've never seen. He was an incredible teammate. And having him in Brooklyn at the start of my coaching journey meant so much. Those who knew him were blessed to call him a friend. You are already missed my brother. Rest in power. 💔

item.title: 76ers tried to make trades for Donte DiVincenzo, Naji Marshall, Ryan Dunn, Brandin Podziemski at deadline
item.description: Among other players the source said the Sixers made runs at, unsuccessfully: Naji Marshall of the Dallas Mavericks, Donte DiVincenzo of the Minnesota Timberwolves, Brandin Podziemski of the Golden State Warriors and Ryan Dunn of the Phoenix Suns. None of those players were moved.

item.title: “I’ve known him for many, many years,” Ujiri said of …
item.description: Masai Ujiri made his first big move last week, when the Mavericks hired Mike Schmitz as general manager. Schmitz got his start working for the independent scouting service DraftExpress. He was an ESPN analyst for five years and then became the Portland Trail Blazers assistant general manager in 2022. Although Portland’s draft success was mixed in Schmitz’s four years there, he helped drive the decision to trade for Deni Avdija, who was named an All-Star this past season. “I’ve known him for many

item.title: Grant Hill on Co-Winning ROY with Jason Kidd: “I got …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Grant Hill on Co-Winning ROY with Jason Kidd: “I got robbed. J Kidd knows that, that’s my guy we came in together. You know, I always joke about it and I always tell him this too. We were teammates at the Nike camp in high school. I don’t think he passed me the ball one time. He was rising sophomore and I’m a rising senior, he supposed to be this great point guard and he never passed me the ball at Nike camp. And then they beat us in the legendary tournament matchup where Cal beat

item.title: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka …
item.description: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka Doncic and LeBron James don’t get along is not true, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal. Doncic and James have a positive relationship and Doncic wants James to re-sign with the Lakers this summer, sources said. While growing up in Slovenia, Doncic looked up to James and values having him as a teammate, sources said.

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a …
item.description: The rumors are true. Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a relationship with Duke University center Arianna Roberson. In a TikTok posted to her account on Thursday, May 14, Roberson, 20, and Flagg, 19, hold hands and embrace in a compilation of clips from their tropical vacation in the the Turks and Caicos Islands.

item.title: Team sources told The Athletic that there were never …
item.description: One bright spot for the Wolves is that they appear set to bring the front-office staff back next year intact. Connelly was a candidate for the top job in Dallas, but team sources told The Athletic that there were never any discussions between the Mavericks and Connelly before Masai Ujiri was hired.

item.title: “Basketball used to be a two-footed sport,” Adams …
item.description: The first clue occurred to me several years ago in a conference room at the Warriors practice facility in San Francisco, during a technical discussion about biomechanics and basketball. At the time, Klay Thompson was still rehabbing from a brutal one-two punch of traumatic lower-body injuries that upended his prime and likely shortened the team’s dynasty. When our meeting wrapped up and the room fell into that unique silence that signals that a group is ready to disperse, Ron Adams, the longtime

item.title: Chandler Parsons when he signed with Dallas: I never wanted to leave Houston
item.description: Chandler Parsons: I wanted to go back and then Mark Cuban offered me the three-year deal in Dallas. And they get like three days or whatever to match through contract if you're a restricted free agent versus an unrestricted free agent. And they didn't did it. They didn't match it. So, like it is bittersweet because like I never wanted to leave Houston. I love Houston. I loved playing for Kevin McCale. I loved playing with James and Dwight.

item.title: Mavs part ways with head coach Jason Kidd
item.description: Mavs PR: The Dallas Mavericks announced today that the team has mutually agreed to part ways with head coach Jason Kidd. The organization will immediately begin a comprehensive search for its next head coach. Full release: https:// nba.com/mavs/news/dall as-mavericks-and-jason-kidd-mutually-agree-to-part-ways

item.title: Sources told ESPN that he has four years and more than …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: The Mavericks announce that they have parted ways with coach Jason Kidd. Sources told ESPN that he has four years and more than $40 million remaining on his contract, but Patrick Dumont authorized Masai Ujiri to make this decision.

item.title: “Jason has had a meaningful impact on the Dallas …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Masai Ujiri on parting ways with Jason Kidd: “Jason has had a meaningful impact on the Dallas Mavericks, both as a Hall of Fame player and as the head coach who helped lead this franchise back to the NBA Finals. We are thankful for Jason’s leadership, his professionalism and his commitment to the team. In my short time here, I’ve developed an enormous amount of respect for what he has built. He will always be an important part of the Mavericks family.”

item.title: Kidd was kept out of the loop in the process that …
item.description: Sources said that Kidd had expressed a desire to be promoted to president of basketball operations after general manager Nico Harrison was fired in November, but Dumont informed Kidd months ago that he wouldn't be considered for the front office. Kidd was kept out of the loop in the process that resulted in Ujiri's hiring, sources said.

item.title: Kidd's frustration with Doncic's frequent conditioning …
item.description: Kidd has insisted that he wasn't aware of the deal until "the 11th hour," doubling down on that explanation in early April after minority owner Mark Cuban appeared on a podcast and accused Kidd of being directly involved in the decision to trade Doncic. Kidd's frustration with Doncic's frequent conditioning issues was well known throughout the organization.

item.title: Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Want to hear something wild? Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure among all NBA coaches, after Jason Kidd's ouster in Dallas. Erik Spoelstra, Steve Kerr, Tyronn Lue, Mark Daigneault, Chris Finch, Rick Carlisle ... and then Will Hardy.

item.title: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020
item.description: Tim Reynolds: 11 coaches have made the NBA Finals since 2020. 6 are no longer with the teams they took to those Finals. 2024: Jason Kidd, Dallas 2023: Michael Malone, Denver 2022: Ime Udoka, Boston 2021: Monty Williams, Phoenix 2021: Mike Budenholzer, Milwaukee 2020: Frank Vogel, Lakers

item.title: Matt Riccardi will not return with the Mavs
item.description: Jake Fischer: I’m told Mavericks executive Matt Riccardi is also departing the organization. Riccardi, who served as Dallas’ co-general manager this season in the wake of Nico Harrison’s exit, was the runner-up to Masai Ujiri in Patrick Dumont’s process to find a new president of basketball operations.

item.title: League sources tell The Stein Line that Kidd and Ujiri …
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that Kidd and Ujiri had multiple conversations over the past two weeks — including a lengthy meeting that included new general manager Mike Schmitz before Ujiri and Schmitz traveled to Chicago for last week's NBA Draft combine — but rumbles nonetheless swirled in the Windy City throughout the combine that a coaching change in Dallas could indeed be imminent.

item.title: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not …
item.description: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not immediately apparent Tuesday night, but San Antonio's Sean Sweeney and Minnesota's Micah Nori — widely regarded around the league as future head coaches — are both known quantities in Dallas.

item.title: Dumont has committed to what industry sources say is …
item.description: Dumont has committed to what industry sources say is comfortably an eight-figure annual salary to land Ujiri and blessed the decision to let Kidd go with so many years and dollars still left on the coach's contract.

item.title: Current minority owner of the Mavericks, Mark Cuban, …
item.description: However, the organization is now moving in a new direction under president Masai Ujiri. "As we evaluate the future of our basketball program, we believe this is the right moment for a new direction for our team," the president said in a statement, as per ESPN. Current minority owner of the Mavericks, Mark Cuban, sent an email to share his thoughts on the news. "Obviously I'm a jkidd fan. So I'm surprised and disappointed," he wrote. "But we have to give masai a chance to see what happens. He has

item.title: Kyrie Irving to draw trade interest?
item.description: Yet there is an anticipation leaguewide that the Mavericks are likely to receive trade interest in Irving this offseason whether they want it or not ... even after the 34-year-old's full season of inactivity.

item.title: Masai Ujiri says Luka Doncic trade played no part in dismissal of Jason Kidd
item.description: Clutch Points: "That trade has played no part in like how I have thought about anything. ... I'm in no position to criticize or blame." Masai Ujiri on whether the Luka Doncic trade played a part in the Mavs moving on from Jason Kidd.

item.title: “I think a new slate was a good way to look at this. I …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says this on the departure of Dallas Mavericks Head Coach: “I think a new slate was a good way to look at this. I feel sometimes in this organization, we needed clarity in where we're going, rather than sometimes lots of things in the mix. We need to literally work in one direction and how we build this team, how we create winning and that's kind of how I looked at it, just going forward and building winning.”

item.title: He shares more, “I don’t think there’s a timeline …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says he doesn’t know how long the Head Coach search will take but it will be a comprehensive search. He shares more, “I don’t think there’s a timeline right now. But it’s something that we want to get done the right way.”

item.title: “Every decision we are going to make here is going to …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri states that the Dallas Mavericks are looking at the future of organization, building around Cooper Flagg. “Every decision we are going to make here is going to be future based. We are looking at the future. We have a 19-year-old on this team that can take us somewhere great. We have to think that way.”

item.title: Masai Ujiri says there is a “huge curiosity” to see …
item.description: Christian Clark: Masai Ujiri says there is a “huge curiosity” to see Kyrie play next to Cooper Flagg.

item.title: Multiple contenders monitoring what happens with Kyrie Irving in Dallas
item.description: Pat McAffee: "The only Mark Cuban guy left on the Mavericks is Kyrie Irving.. There's multiple contenders around the NBA that are very much monitoring what happens with Kyrie Irving in Dallas" ~ @ShamsCharania

item.title: Mavs sources believe that Jason Kidd had a level of …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Masai Ujiri has full authority to do whatever he wants with the direction of this Mavericks team.. Mavs sources believe that Jason Kidd had a level of support for the Luka Doncic trade.. This is a complete restart for the entire organization" ~ @ShamsCharania

item.title: Masai Ujiri on Mavericks head coaching search: First-time, experienced, college, European, every stone will be unturned
item.description: Mike Curtis: Masai Ujiri on how much previous head coaching experience will play a role into the hire of a new coach: “It’s an open search…Out minds are very open. We’re going to look everywhere. Every stone unturned and we will really, really look at what’s best for this organization. First-time, experienced, college, European, everywhere. Whatever is best for this organization going forward.”

item.title: Masai Ujiri says that “nobody has received a phone …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri says that “nobody has received a phone call from him” in search of a new Head Coach, he felt it was disrespectful to Jason Kidd to speak with other candidates at this time.

item.title: “Kevin Durant once told me that there's only one Kyrie …
item.description: Abby Jones: Masai Ujiri speaks on the future of Kyrie Irving in Dallas after the departure of HC, Jason Kidd. “Kevin Durant once told me that there's only one Kyrie Irving walking around in the world. I think we have to figure out a way, how Kyrie fits with our program. I've had those conversations with Kyrie, up till yesterday and I think Kyrie will fit. There's a huge curiosity in our minds to see how Kyrie fits playing with Cooper Flagg … and I think we owe that to this organization.”

item.title: But when I look at the Trail Blazers roster, that …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Jason Kidd from Oakland. Damian Lillard also from Oakland. Point guard. You look at Scoot Henderson, a young guard. How can Jason Kidd help him? So when you take a step back and you look at those jobs, I definitely can see Jason Kidd taking the Trail Blazers job. Now, however, it's been widely known that the owner of their team doesn't want to pay the head coach. It's been widely publicized. A lot of people have been talking about it. And quite honestly, you're making $10 millio

item.title: There are rumors flying around, and I'm not going to …
item.description: Tim McMahon: "I would say a couple things. There are rumors flying around, and I'm not going to say, there's a big-name college coach being rumored. Masai's track record is not necessarily swinging for big names. It's trying to find the next great coach. He likes discoveries."

item.title: Nowitzki also has the record for most regular-season …
item.description: Fun fact: Nowitzki also has the record for most regular-season wins without All-NBA teammates with 804. Dallas fans should be grateful to this day that Nowitzki was loyal enough to never ask out, because a lot of other stars without star help surely would have gotten frustrated enough to demand a trade.

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: Shams: Multiple contenders are keeping an eye on Kyrie Irving right now
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are remaking their franchise around young star Cooper Flagg, which naturally leads to questions about how Kyrie Irving fits into that mix. "Multiple contenders are keeping an eye on Irving right now," ESPN's Shams Charania reported on Wednesday's NBA Today (2:38 mark). "He's got a guaranteed year left on his deal and then he's got a player option, so that does give him some leverage on his future. He's 34 years old, he's a championship player in this league, but he's got a l

item.title: Jason Kidd pens goodbye to Dallas
item.description: Jason Kidd: Thank you, Dallas.

item.title: NBA exec says Dawn Staley could be candidate for Mavs head coaching job
item.description: Dawn Staley later said that going through that process made her believe that “no NBA team is ready for a female coach right now.” It’s unclear if Staley would still consider a move to the NBA after that experience. Ujiri interviewed Hammon — the head coach of the WNBA’s Las Vegas Aces — for the Raptors job three years ago. One NBA executive told The Athletic that it wouldn’t be shocking if the Mavericks wanted to interview Staley.

item.title: Rival executives monitoring Kyrie Irving's long-term fit alongside Cooper Flagg
item.description: “Durant once told me that there’s only one Kyrie Irving walking around in the world,” Ujiri said most recently. “Yeah, I think we have to figure out a way how Kyrie fits with our program. I’ve had those conversations with Kyrie. I think Kyrie will fit. There’s a huge curiosity in our minds to see how Kyrie fits playing with Cooper Flagg. He’s just that kind of like an incredible talent and player. I think we owe this organization that.” Yet, Ujiri’s adjoining stance that Flagg and Irving can for

item.title: Mike Schmitz on Kyrie Irving and Cooper Flagg: 'It's a match made in Heaven'
item.description: Notwithstanding, new Mavericks general manager Mike Schmitz echoed Ujiri’s sentiment and lauded the pairing of Cooper Flagg and Irving on paper when looking ahead to the upcoming season. “That’s something you dream of,” Schmitz said. “Having a magician with the ball like that who can pass, dribble, shoot, and someone with the connective qualities of Cooper. It’s a match made in Heaven.”

item.title: Thus, while the stars may not align on paper for a …
item.description: It’s worth noting that former Dallas Mavericks head coach Jason Kidd has quietly been connected to Portland within league circles. Yet, will that chatter lead to anything more substantive now or in the future? Now, Portland’s current price range for a head coach is well below Kidd’s remaining contract with the Mavericks, which is for four years and $40 million, according to ESPN. Kidd also expressed a desire to make front office personnel decisions before Dallas hired Masai Ujiri and Portland g

item.title: Lakers and Mavericks interested in 76ers’ Prosper Karangwa
item.description: While Nelson appears to be the safest of any current 76ers executive on staff, it’s unclear what’s next for others on the staff. For example, Prosper Karangwa has drawn interest from the Lakers and Mavericks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Mavericks interested in adding Patrick Englebrecht, Prosper Karangwa to their front office
item.description: According to league sources, the Mavericks have expressed interest in adding Philadelphia’s Prosper Karangwa to their front office, and Toronto’s director of global scouting, Patrick Englebrecht, is also a candidate to reunite with Ujiri in Dallas after they worked together since the 2013-14 season.

item.title: Charlie Villanueva doesn't have Shai Gilgeous-Alexander in his Top 10 point guards ever
item.description: Charlie Villanueva on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: I wouldn't even put him in the top 10. Q. Give me 10 point guards, combo point guards, it doesn’t matter. Villanueva: Magic Johnson, Isiah Thomas, Stephen Curry, Allen Iverson, John Stockton… I wouldn't put him up past John Stockton, fam. Q. But that's only five you've named so far. Villanueva: Okay, let me get there! Steve Nash, Chris Paul… Okay. I'm cool. I'm cool with all these… Oh, also Tony Parker. (…) He’s not in front of Jason Kidd. (…) Bob C

item.title: Dallas a long-time intriguing destination for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that there is at least one destination in the West that has long intrigued Giannis Antetokounmpo but which rarely gets mentioned. Dallas. The interest began to bubble years ago, of course, thanks to the presence of the Luka Dončić in The 214 and the tantalizing scenario of Giannis teaming up with Luka.

item.title: I’m told that the Mavericks actually did do some …
item.description: Antetokounmpo and Kidd have remained close even though they haven't worked together since the Bucks fired Kidd in January 2018 ... and I’m told that the Mavericks actually did do some behind-the-scenes investigating leading up to the February trade deadline to try to find out if they still appeal to the two-time MVP.

item.title: Kyrie Irving to Lakers not likely to happen?
item.description: As far as the Los Angeles Lakers' chances of pulling a potential deal around Irving, Fischer dismissed speculations that the franchise will try to reunite the star point guard with Luka Doncic in the wake of Austin Reaves' free-agency situation. "My only real understanding of what the Lakers want to do from a timeline perspective is they want to bring in a talent that fits 27-year-old Luka Doncic's timeline. As great as that pairing of Luka and Kyrie was, I think Kyrie is a little bit redundant

item.title: Magic to hire Sean Sweeney as head coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic will hire Sean Sweeney as their head coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Sweeney was an associate head coach for the San Antonio Spurs. Prior, he was an assistant coach for the Dallas Mavericks, Detroit Pistons, Milwaukee Bucks, and Brooklyn Nets.

item.title: “I can say he’s been doing everything humanly possible …
item.description: Dallas Mavericks general manager Mike Schmitz said he has seen Kyrie Irving doing everything possible to return at full strength from the torn ACL that ended his 2025-26 season, citing the 34-year-old guard’s presence and approach inside the team’s practice facility this offseason. Schmitz made the comments Friday during a roundtable with reporters at the American Airlines Center. “I can say he’s been doing everything humanly possible to maximize his talent and to do that,” Schmitz said. “Just t

item.title: Luka Doncic, Donnie Nelson acquire Italian club to submit NBA Europe bid
item.description: Did he want to help move a professional basketball team to Rome? The question came from Donnie Nelson, a former Mavericks executive who had brought Mr. Doncic to the National Basketball Association from Europe and was among those celebrating the win. Mr. Doncic barely had to think before answering yes. The pair are now the lead decision makers of an investment group that has bought a northern Italian basketball team, Vanoli Cremona, from a town that is famous for its violin makers. The group pla

item.title: “My mom, when she immigrated here, she understood the …
item.description: A New Jersey native, Towns also explained why he chose the Knicks over the Nets. “My mom, when she immigrated here, she understood the magnitude of Madison Square Garden,” Towns said. “… I grew up, obviously, in Jersey, obviously it was so much easier for me to get New Jersey Nets tickets and see Kenyon Martin, Vince Carter, Jason Kidd, but my family has always had such respect and love for the Knicks and my father having ties to the Knicks, it was kind of guaranteed I was going to fall into thi

item.title: Kyrie Irving says he is close to 100 percent in his ACL recovery
item.description: Mike Curtis: Kyrie Irving: “I am definitely close to being over at 100% in terms of my ACL recovery. It’s been a while now…I’m so grateful that I’ve had the time to heal & just experiment with my body more on the court and off the court in the weight room & just pushing myself to the limit.”

item.title: "Mike Brown at best was the sixth choice for this job.
item.description: Marc Stein: "Mike Brown at best was the sixth choice for this job. Remember that the Knicks asked for permission to speak to Jason Kidd, Ime Udoka, Quinn Snyder, Chris Finch, and Billy Donovan. All of those teams said no. Jason Kidd and Billy Donovan both got contract extensions and no longer coach the teams where they were a year ago. It really looked like the Knicks fired Tom Thibodeau without a plan to replace the first coach who had taken them to the conference finals this century. For thing

item.title: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Who are your top three defensive teammates of all time? Andrew Bogut number one, Klay Thompson number two. Klay Thompson, ball hawk. And I'd say number three, number three, my favorite defensive teammate of all time, or not favorite, but top three. Kevin Durant was elite defensively here. Elite. Andre Iguodala. Andre Iguodala made the game really easy. Got to go with Andre. It has to be Andre."

item.title: Derek Harper expects Luka Doncic to retire with the Mavericks
item.description: Derek Harper: "I love Nico, Nico is my buddy, but I would not want my name on that trade if I ever had anything to do with the NBA. You look back, you gave away what, 30 points, eight and nine, you gave it to somebody else for a guy that is no longer on your roster. That one is hard to get around. It is just something at the time I think they thought about doing and they were quick to react, and it just did not work out, especially here in Dallas. People love Luka here. I think he will eventuall

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement.

item.title: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say …
item.description: Multiple people close to Mavericks governor Dumont say he grew increasingly leery of becoming the face of a tear-down-City Hall movement, especially since it wasn’t the Mavericks’ idea in the first place. Along with availability, multiple people close to negotiations said the Mavericks had logistical concerns about the City Hall site’s ingress and egress on game and concert nights.

item.title: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa …
item.description: There is a growing anticipation that Prosper Karangwa will elect to stay with the 76ers after the hiring of Mike Gansey as the team's new president of basketball operations. Karangwa is the Sixers' vice president of player personnel and has drawn interest in recent weeks from the Los Angeles Lakers and Dallas.

item.title: The Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major …
item.description: After more than a year of analyzing Dallas sites for their planned multi-billion-dollar arena and entertainment district, the Mavericks have chosen Valley View, dealing a major setback to efforts to bring the project downtown. The Mavericks on Monday executed option agreements to purchase approximately 104 acres of the 110-acre former site of Valley View Mall, the franchise told The Dallas Morning News in an emailed statement. It’s a seismic decision that could reshape Dallas’ sports and enterta

item.title: That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s …
item.description: The Mavericks were seeking at least a 50-acre footprint for the project, the centerpiece being a basketball-specific arena to be completed by the time its American Airlines Center lease expires on July 28, 2031. That timeline was a significant factor in Monday’s decision, multiple people familiar with the negotiating process told The News.

item.title: Dereck Lively: "My mom is the first person, first …
item.description: Dereck Lively: "My mom is the first person, first coach. She used to coach me to kick me out of a gym. She kicked me out of practice because I called her mom, and then I had to sit the entire rest of practice outside, couldn't get in the car, I was locked out of the gym, and I just had to sit on the curb. She was a loving person, but when it came to basketball, she knew everything around the game. She taught me many things, no matter if it's, as soon as you start a commitment, you got to finish

item.title: Kyrie Irving's time in Dallas running out?
item.description: In his latest Bleacher Report livestream, NBA insider Jake Fischer argued that he does not fully expect the star point guard to remain in Dallas into the next 2026-27 season. “I don’t necessarily believe that Kyrie Irving will be on the Mavericks roster come opening night," Fischer said. "There will be contending teams that would make trade calls to Dallas.”

item.title: On Wednesday, Obi Agbim, Michael Ajayi, Christian …
item.description: Dallas enters the June 23-24 draft with three picks: Nos. 9, 30 and 48. On Wednesday, Obi Agbim, Michael Ajayi, Christian Anderson, Tucker DeVries and Wyatt Fricks became the latest players to work out at the Mavericks’ practice facility, Agbim told The Dallas Morning News.

item.title: Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose …
item.description: Mike Curtis: #Mavs forward Naji Marshall is in San Antonio for tonight’s Game 1 between the #Knicks and #Spurs. Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose Alvarado, his former teammate of three seasons in New Orleans.

item.title: “And then, obviously, Nico is talking trash behind the …
item.description: Mark Cuban: “I mean, like, me going to Patrick: ‘Okay, Patrick, what’s going on?’ “He’s going to give you a report, right? Every two weeks, you get a report, and you sit down. Then, ‘Oh, yeah, I want you to talk more with Nico. Get down there with Nico.’ “And then, obviously, Nico is talking trash behind the scenes. But again, we’re winning. I don’t want to create a problem when things are going well. “And if we had been losing, I probably would have stepped forward and said, ‘Look, this is not

item.title: Mark Cuban accuses Nico Harrison of taking credit for the Kyrie Irving deal
item.description: Interviewer: “So, when you say ‘talking trash behind the scenes,’ like politically maneuvering? Going to maybe Patrick and saying, ‘Hey, I’m the guy doing this, not Mark’?” Mark Cuban: “Absolutely.” Interviewer: “What is he doing to you? What is he saying? How do you find out about—” Mark Cuban: “Like, ‘Oh, I did the Kyrie deal. He didn’t do the Kyrie deal.’ “Right. Yeah, he might have talked to the general manager, but I’m the one that made the decision. I talked to the agent. I talked to the o

item.title: Mark Cuban: "Masai and Nico are a lot alike, but …
item.description: Mark Cuban: "Masai and Nico are a lot alike, but they're different in the important ways. Nico wanted all his people in there, and you saw how many people he got rid of and who he got rid of. Masai's a lot of the same way. But Nico didn't really know what he was doing. I hired him to work for me, right, not to run the the the basketball operations."

item.title: "I had gotten a Tesla and I said something that I …
item.description: Mark Cuban on Elon Musk: "I had gotten a Tesla and I said something that I didn't like about it. And he just texted me and said, 'Fuck you.'"
